task_completion.description: Verify that Thunderbird has been put into Offline Mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the offline indicator in Thunderbird, typically located in the bottom-left corner of the window (e.g., an icon showing two disconnected screens or a plug with a red cross), or a menu showing 'Work Offline' is checked.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct recipient is entered in the composition window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the email composition window, the 'To' field must contain exactly 'executive.team@company.com'.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct subject line is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the email composition window, the 'Subject' field must contain exactly 'Q3 Analysis Complete'.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct email body text is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the email composition window, the main text area must contain the required text: 'The Q3 analysis is complete. I will upload the documents when I have a better connection.'

task_completion.description: Verify the email was queued using the 'Send Later' function.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the email appearing in the 'Outbox' or 'Unsent Messages' folder in the left pane of Thunderbird, or a visible interaction with the 'File > Send Later' menu option (or shortcut Ctrl+Shift+Return). The email must be queued, not sent.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Thunderbird GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Mozilla Thunderbird application interface being used, not a plain text editor or web browser.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use the 'Save as Draft' function.
integrity.visual_evidence: The email should not appear in the 'Drafts' folder, and there should be no evidence of clicking 'Save' or 'Save as Draft'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to send or queue the email.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like 'mail', 'sendmail', or script executions should be used to bypass the Thunderbird GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Recipient: 'executive.team@company.com'. Expected Subject: 'Q3 Analysis Complete'. Expected Body Keywords: 'Q3 analysis is complete' and 'better connection'.
